Zoom in,
to zoom out.

Connect the data your organisation already has and make clear decisions.

Prepared for Dawn Tittensor  ·  Confidential  ·  May 2025
lens.ai
The problem
Data is everywhere. But without Lens, it's like trying to read without your glasses. Blurry. Unclear. Out of focus.

Organisations spend millions on LinkedIn Talent Insights, HCM platforms, ATS systems and market intelligence tools. And then — when the CHRO walks into the board meeting — they rely on recruiter feedback to answer the questions that actually matter.


The data exists. It has always existed. What has never existed is a way to bring it all together, make it interrogatable, and deliver it as a decision — not a dashboard.

"Is our business the right shape for future opportunities?"
That question has an answer. It has always had an answer.
It just never had the infrastructure to surface it. Until now.
$9.9B
Global HR analytics market by 2031 — growing at 14.9% CAGR
91%
of CHROs say AI & digitisation is their most immediate concern in 2026
£2B+
Addressable market globally — private sector, public sector, NHS, universities
Zero
Products that combine all data sources into one auto-generated board brief
The solution

A Chief Intelligence Officer
that never sleeps.

Lens is a strategic decision engine — not an HR reporting tool. It synthesises nine data sources into a single executive brief, delivered automatically every month before anyone has to ask for it. Between briefs, any authorised user can open Lens and ask any question. The answer comes back in seconds, anchored entirely in their own data.


Most analytics tools require the user to ask the right question. Lens answers the right question before it is asked.

📋
The Monthly Brief
A four-to-six page board-ready intelligence document. The Market This Month. Your Position. The Threats. The Recommendations. Auto-generated. Same format every month. No analyst required.
💬
Always-On Interrogation
"Where are competitors outmanoeuvring us?" "Can we resource our Q3 bids?" "Which roles need a salary review?" Answers in seconds. Source-referenced. Defensible in any board meeting.
📄
The Shareable Summary
A one-page auto-generated executive summary for the CEO or CFO who needs the three headline findings before the meeting starts. Ready to download and share instantly.
The data architecture

Nine sources. One picture.
One answer.

Lens connects what your organisation already has. Each source adds a layer of intelligence. Together they answer the question no single tool can touch.

1
LinkedIn Talent Insights
External talent market, competitor hiring, skills demand, pool depth by location and function
2
ATS data
Internal pipeline health, time to hire, source quality, offer acceptance rates
3
HCM & HR data
Attrition, headcount, hiring velocity, internal capability and skills mapping
4
Workforce planning data
Headcount targets, growth forecasts, role demand by location and function
5
Bid & pipeline data
Live bids, contracted projects, delivery timelines, skills required to fulfil them
6
Market intelligence
Competitor news, project wins and losses, industry trends, regulatory developments
7
Financial & investor signals
Share price, analyst sentiment, earnings context — the boardroom pressure behind every decision
8
Employer brand signals
Indeed reviews, job posting volume, LinkedIn company signals vs competitors
9
Market compensation data
Salary benchmarks by role and location — whether you can attract and retain the talent you need
Lens works from day one with what you have. Every additional source makes the brief richer and the answers sharper. You decide the pace.
Orchestrated AI intelligence
Unlike tools that rely on a single AI model, Lens uses an orchestrated ensemble — multiple AI models working in sequence, each peer-reviewing the others' outputs. GPT-4o opens the analysis, Gemini builds on it, Claude synthesises the final answer. The result is more reliable, more nuanced intelligence than any single model can deliver alone. The same principle that makes trading algorithms more robust makes Lens more trustworthy at the moment it matters most.
The platform

Clean. Dynamic. Decision-ready.

Six tabs. Every data source in its place. A live AI analyst. A monthly brief that writes itself. Click through the tabs below — this is the actual platform, not a screenshot.

Proof of concept

Built in 48 hours.
Mistaken for a production platform.

The Lens prototype was built against 25 real LinkedIn Talent Insights reports from a major global energy company — combined with live competitor news feeds, industry signals and real-time share price data. The AI analyst interrogates all of it simultaneously and surfaces intelligence no single tool could produce alone.

"
What the hell is that — how do I get access?
Senior TA leader, major European energy company — on seeing the prototype for the first time, assuming it was an established enterprise production platform
The compounding advantage

Month one delivers intelligence.
Month twelve delivers institutional memory.

Month 1
First intelligence
The first brief is generated. Market signals, competitor movement, internal position. For the first time, the CHRO walks into the board meeting with answers rather than slides.
Month 3
Patterns emerge
Lens begins connecting signals across months. Attrition trends. Competitor acceleration. Salary gaps widening before they become visible problems. Early warnings, not post-mortems.
Month 6
Predictive intelligence
Six months of context. Lens can now say not just what is happening — but what will happen, and what to do about it before the window closes.
Month 12
Institutional memory
A full year of intelligence that cannot be replicated or rebuilt retrospectively. Cancelling Lens doesn't mean losing a subscription. It means losing a year of irreplaceable strategic context.
That is not stickiness.
That is adhesive.
Once Lens is embedded in the monthly board cycle, walking away is not a cancellation. It is a strategic retreat.
<10%
Annual churn — because the cost of leaving
exceeds the cost of staying
Competitive landscape

Why not Visier, Workday,
or LinkedIn Talent Insights?

Each solves one part of the problem. None connect to your bid pipeline. None produce a board-ready brief. None synthesise across all nine sources into a single proactive answer. Lens doesn't compete with these tools — it sits on top of them. Visier starts at $35,000 per year for internal analytics only. Mercer and Korn Ferry require consultants and cost six figures. Lens does more, for less, automatically.

The market

The person Lens is built for.

The addressable market is far broader than enterprise energy and engineering. Every organisation with a board, a people function and a data problem is a potential customer — private sector, public sector, NHS, housing associations, universities, professional services, financial services.

The global HR analytics market is valued at $9.9 billion and growing at 14.9% CAGR. At £30,000 per year, Lens's global addressable market exceeds £2 billion. The immediate UK and EU enterprise sweet spot represents over 50,000 organisations.

Lens tells it for her.

Catherine
CHRO · Global Engineering · 25 years experience
"Every month end I pull together a report I know doesn't tell the full story. I have the data — somewhere — but by the time I've found it, cleaned it and made it presentable, the board meeting has already started."
With Lens, Catherine walks into the June board with a five-page intelligence brief that tells the board not just what happened — but what's coming, where the risk sits, and what to do before it's too late.

For the first time, she's not chasing her tail. She's driving the conversation. She's the most informed person in the room.

Focus. Finally.
The pilot proposition

Let us build it for you.
You keep the results.

"You've spent £1.4 million on a LinkedIn contract. Let us make it actually work for you."

The Lens founding team engages as LTD company contractors via your existing RHI payroll infrastructure. No procurement process. No software purchase. No committee.

1
Intelligence foundation
We configure the intelligence layer around your specific priorities, critical roles, key competitors and target markets.
2
Data connection
LinkedIn TI reports, Oracle HCM, Power BI exports, ATS pipeline, competitor news feeds — all connected through a clean self-serve panel.
3
First brief — week four
The first board-ready intelligence brief is generated. Real data. Real insights. Shared with CHRO and leadership for feedback.
4
Iterate with stakeholders
We work with your CHRO, HR Services and WFP teams to ensure the brief answers the questions that actually matter.
5
Delivery & testimonial
Platform delivered. Named video testimonials from CHRO, technology and business leadership. All IP stays with Lens.

The team

Built by people who lived
the problem for decades.

Lens was not built by technologists who spotted a market gap. It was built by two people who spent 13 years inside one of the world's largest engineering organisations — and left knowing exactly what was missing.
